---
title: "CANOE 16s: "
author: "Elad Deiss-Yehiely"
date: "July 2025"
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
    toc: yes
  pdf_document:
    toc: yes
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---


```{r load-packages, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
required_packages <- c(
  "dplyr", "ggplot2", "lmerTest", "phyloseq", "tidyverse", 
  "parallel", "vegan", "ggtext", "ggpubr", "reshape2", "RColorBrewer", "ggrepel", "circlize", "SummarizedExperiment", "kableExtra", "compositions", "knitr", "DESeq2", "pheatmap", "cluster", "data.table"
)
sapply(required_packages, require, character.only = TRUE)


```

### 1.) Import the 16s Phyloseq

```{r}
phy_3Runs <- readRDS("/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE/3_Runs_Merged/dada2_phy_pruned_wtree_3Runs.rds")
phy_3Runs
### AM REMOVING DUPLICATE TIMEPOINTS FROM SEQUENCING ###
phy_3Runs <- subset_samples(phy_3Runs, !(sample_names(phy_3Runs) %in% c("VAUN000542", "UWIM101853", "VAUN004162")))
phy_3Runs


formatted_metadata <- read.csv("/Users/ediessy/Desktop/formatted_merged_metadata_edited_2026-03-30_EDY.csv", header=T, row.names = 1)
# colnames(formatted_metadata) <- gsub("[^[:alnum:]_]", ".", colnames(formatted_metadata))  
# colnames(formatted_metadata) <- gsub("\\.+", ".", colnames(formatted_metadata)) 
# colnames(formatted_metadata) <- sub("\\.$", "", colnames(formatted_metadata))

# #Want to merge in data later once all the filtering has been completed so that you don't have to deal with MOCK, NEC, NTC(?)
#sample_data(phy_3Runs) <- sample_data(formatted_metadata)


CANOE_phy_3Runs <- prune_samples(sample_data(phy_3Runs)$Study%in% c("CANOE_Airway_16S", "CANOE_Airway_16S_Run3"), phy_3Runs)
CANOE_phy_3Runs <- prune_samples(sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs)$Extractor %in% c("Kris_Grindle"), CANOE_phy_3Runs)

# Remove samples with zero reads
CANOE_phy_3Runs <- prune_samples(sample_sums(CANOE_phy_3Runs) > 0, CANOE_phy_3Runs)
CANOE_phy_3Runs <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(CANOE_phy_3Runs) > 0, CANOE_phy_3Runs)
CANOE_phy_3Runs

#First pass filter on all non-bacteria taxa
CANOE_phy_3Runs <- CANOE_phy_3Runs %>% subset_taxa(Kingdom == "Bacteria") #15856 taxa and 926 samples prior to sub-setting, 15668 post
CANOE_phy_3Runs <- CANOE_phy_3Runs %>% subset_taxa( Family!= "Mitochondria" | is.na(Family) & Order!="Chloroplast" | is.na(Order)) #15257 taxa
taxa_names(CANOE_phy_3Runs) <- paste0('ASV_', 1:length(taxa_names(CANOE_phy_3Runs)))
phy_tree(CANOE_phy_3Runs) <- ape::root(phy_tree(CANOE_phy_3Runs), 1, resolve.root=T)
CANOE_phy_3Runs <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(CANOE_phy_3Runs) > 0, CANOE_phy_3Runs)
```


###Negative Control Filtering###

```{r}
#NTC removal was already done for each individual run, whereas NEC removal was not
#Manual addition of a column SampleType for NEC, NTC, MOCK, Sample in the metadata table. Will probably need to do this with any changes (like adding in new data, though there you should just add it to the optimized/finalized phyloseq object).
source("/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE/Phylo_Tree/NegControlRemoval_func.R")
CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg <- ntc_filter(CANOE_phy_3Runs)
CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg #14662 taxa and 905 samples (14 MOCK and 891 sample, which is 297 complete longitudinal children).


CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg <- subset_samples(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg, SampleType == "sample")

#Remember that HFHS001257 was removed as there were not enough reads in the dada2 processing stage
formatted_metadata <- formatted_metadata[rownames(formatted_metadata) != "HFHS001257", ]

#Needs to be character(0) to make sure that the merge can happen properly!
base::setdiff(rownames(formatted_metadata), sample_names(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg))
base::setdiff(sample_names(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg), rownames(formatted_metadata))


sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg) <- sample_data(formatted_metadata)


```

###Alpha-Diversity###

```{r}
#Plotted both with and without total sum scaling, should give similar results but want to be thorough. TSS does not add much and will only do non-transformed data here to include the Chao-1

# Step 1: Extract alpha diversity metrics (already done)
otu_data <- as.matrix(otu_table(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg))
phy_tree <- phy_tree(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)

sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)$Chao1 <- t(estimateR(otu_data))[, 2] #Cannot use with TSS, it needs to be counts of unique taxa
sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)$Shannon <- vegan::diversity(otu_data, index = "shannon")
sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)$Pileou_Evenness <- vegan::diversity(otu_data) / log(specnumber(otu_data))
sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)$Faith_PD <- picante::pd(otu_data, phy_tree, include.root = TRUE)[, 1]

# Step 2: Convert sample_data to data.frame
alpha_data <- as.data.frame(sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg))

# Step 3: Convert Age variable to a factor or ordered factor
# (Optional but useful for plotting)
alpha_data$Age <- as.factor(alpha_data$Age)  # or use ordered() if Age is numeric

# Step 4: Melt alpha diversity metrics into long format
alpha_long_age <- reshape2::melt(
  alpha_data,
  id.vars = "Age",
  measure.vars = c("Chao1", "Shannon", "Pileou_Evenness", "Faith_PD"),
  variable.name = "Metric",
  value.name = "Value"
)

# Step 5: Remove rows with missing values
alpha_long_age <- alpha_long_age[!is.na(alpha_long_age$Value), ]

# Loop over metrics and run pairwise Wilcoxon tests
metrics <- unique(alpha_long_age$Metric)

for (m in metrics) {
  cat("=== Wilcoxon Tests for", m, "===\n")
  sub_data <- alpha_long_age[alpha_long_age$Metric == m, ]
  
  result <- pairwise.wilcox.test(
    x = sub_data$Value,
    g = sub_data$Age,
    p.adjust.method = "BH"  # Benjamini-Hochberg adjustment
  )
  
  print(result)
  cat("\n")
}

age_comparisons <- combn(levels(alpha_long_age$Age), 2, simplify = FALSE)

# Step 6: Plot by Age
alpha_plot_age <- ggplot(alpha_long_age, aes(x = Age, y = Value, fill = Age)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = 21, outlier.fill = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~Metric, scales = "free", ncol = 2) +
  labs(x = "Age (Months)", y = "Alpha Diversity", fill = "Age") +
  ggtitle("Alpha Diversity Metrics by Age") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Age")) +
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = age_comparisons,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    label = "p.signif",
    hide.ns = FALSE,     # Show comparisons that are not significant (Use TRUE to hide them)
    step.increase = 0.2 # Increase vertical spacing of comparisons
  )

# View the plot
alpha_plot_age


```

### Prevalence filtering graph (can do a few different filters) and normalization, TSS, log(TSS+pseudocount), CLR
```{r}
#CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg is the phyloseq object currently!

#  Calculating Prevalence, plotting each phylum to justify different prevalence cutoffs
prevdf <- apply(X = otu_table(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg),
                MARGIN = ifelse(taxa_are_rows(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg), yes = 1, no = 2),
                FUN = function(x) { sum(x > 0) })
prevdf <- data.frame(Prevalence = prevdf,
                     TotalAbundance = taxa_sums(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg),
                     as.data.frame(tax_table(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)))
  prevdf1 <- subset(prevdf, Phylum %in% get_taxa_unique(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg, "Phylum"))
  p.prevdf1 <- ggplot(prevdf1, aes(TotalAbundance, Prevalence / nsamples(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg), color = Genus)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.1, alpha = 0.5, linetype = 2) +
    geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_x_log10() +
    xlab("Total Abundance") +
    ylab("Prevalence [Frac. Samples]") +
    facet_wrap(~Phylum) +
    theme(legend.position = "none") +
    ggtitle("Phylum Prevalence in All Samples\nColored by Genus")
  print(p.prevdf1)
```

### Will try and rarefy here ###
```{r}
#Take CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg and create a plot of unique ASV per read depth to get a proper cutoff for rarefaction.
otu_3runs_noneg <- as.data.frame(otu_table(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg))
rare_list <- rarecurve(otu_3runs_noneg, step = 100, sample = min(rowSums(otu_3runs_noneg)), label = FALSE)

# Convert to dataframe for ggplot
rare_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(rare_list), function(i) {
  data.frame(
    SampleID = rownames(otu_3runs_noneg)[i],
    Depth    = attr(rare_list[[i]], "Subsample"),
    Richness = rare_list[[i]]
  )
}))

ggplot(rare_df, aes(x = Depth, y = Richness, group = SampleID, color = SampleID)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1000, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 5000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 400)) +
  labs(x = "Read Depth", y = "ASV Richness",
       title = "Rarefaction Curves",
       #subtitle = "Red line = candidate rarefaction depth") 
       )+
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")  # remove legend - too many samples to label usefully

#Rarefy once to get a distribution:
# Define rarefaction depths to test
depths <- c(500, 1000, 2000, 3000)

# Check total samples before rarefaction
cat("Total samples before rarefaction:", nsamples(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg), "\n\n")

# Loop through each depth
for (d in depths) {
  
  # Identify which samples fall below this depth
  sample_depths <- sample_sums(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)
  dropped <- sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)[sample_depths < d, ]
  kept <- sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)[sample_depths >= d, ]
  
  cat("=== Rarefaction depth:", d, "===\n")
  cat("Samples retained:", nrow(kept), "\n")
  cat("Samples dropped:", nrow(dropped), "\n")
  
  if (nrow(dropped) > 0) {
    cat("Dropped samples by Group:\n")
    print(table(dropped$Group))  # replace Group with your actual column name
  } else {
    cat("No samples dropped\n")
  }
  
  cat("Retained samples by Group:\n")
  print(table(kept$Group))  # replace Group with your actual column name
  cat("\n")
}

ps_rarefied_single <- rarefy_even_depth(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg,
                                  sample.size = 1000,  # replace with your chosen depth
                                  rngseed = 481516,
                                  replace = FALSE,
                                  trimOTUs = TRUE,
                                  verbose = TRUE)

ps_1_rarefy <- phyloseq(ps_rarefied_single <- phyloseq(
  otu_table(ps_rarefied),                        # rarefied OTU table
  sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg),            # original metadata
  tax_table(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg),              # original taxonomy
  phy_tree(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)                # original tree
))


#then rarefy 100 times to compare. Choose the medoid to use as the distribution (more central to all others).
set.seed(481516)  # for reproducibility
n_reps <- 100
depth <- 1000  # replace with your chosen depth

# Store OTU tables from each rarefaction
rarefied_list <- vector("list", n_reps)

for (i in 1:n_reps) {
  ps_rare_canoe <- rarefy_even_depth(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg, 
                                sample.size = depth,
                                rngseed = i,        # different seed each iteration
                                replace = FALSE,
                                trimOTUs = FALSE,
                                verbose = FALSE)
  rarefied_list[[i]] <- matrix(
  as.integer(as.matrix(otu_table(ps_rare_canoe))),
  nrow = ntaxa(ps_rare_canoe),
  dimnames = list(taxa_names(ps_rare_canoe), sample_names(ps_rare_canoe))
)
}

cat("Done rarefying\n")

# Get sample names from first iteration
sample_names_list <- colnames(rarefied_list[[1]])  # adjust if taxa_are_rows differs
n_samples <- length(sample_names_list)

# For each sample, find the rarefaction closest to the center of all 100
medoid_otu <- matrix(0, 
                     nrow = nrow(rarefied_list[[1]]), 
                     ncol = n_samples,
                     dimnames = list(rownames(rarefied_list[[1]]), 
                                     sample_names_list))

for (s in seq_along(sample_names_list)) {
  
  samp <- sample_names_list[s]
  
  # Extract this sample's profile across all 100 rarefactions
  # Each row = one rarefaction, each column = one ASV
  profiles <- do.call(rbind, lapply(rarefied_list, function(x) x[, samp]))
  
  # Compute Bray-Curtis distance between all 100 profiles for this sample
  # Use Bray-Curtis for abundance, or "jaccard" for presence/absence
  dist_matrix <- as.matrix(vegdist(profiles, method = "bray"))
  
  # Find medoid: the profile with minimum total distance to all others
  total_dist <- rowSums(dist_matrix)
  medoid_idx <- which.min(total_dist)
  
  # Store that profile
  medoid_otu[, samp] <- profiles[medoid_idx, ]
}

cat("Medoid selection complete\n")

asv_sums <- rowSums(medoid_otu)
cat("ASVs before zero removal:", nrow(medoid_otu), "\n")

medoid_otu_filtered <- medoid_otu[asv_sums > 0, ]
cat("ASVs after zero removal:", nrow(medoid_otu_filtered), "\n")

# Get surviving ASV names for subsetting tax_table and tree
surviving_asvs <- rownames(medoid_otu_filtered)

# Now rebuild phyloseq with filtered medoid_otu
surviving_samples <- colnames(medoid_otu_filtered)
sample_data_filtered <- sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)[surviving_samples, ]

ps_100_rarefied_medoid <- phyloseq(
  otu_table(medoid_otu_filtered, taxa_are_rows = TRUE),
  sample_data_filtered,
  tax_table(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)[surviving_asvs, ],
  prune_taxa(surviving_asvs, phy_tree(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg))
)


# Verify dimensions all match
cat("Samples in OTU table:", ncol(medoid_otu_filtered), "\n")
cat("Samples in sample_data:", nrow(sample_data_filtered), "\n")
cat("ASVs in OTU table:", nrow(medoid_otu_filtered), "\n")
cat("ASVs in tax_table:", ntaxa(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg), "\n")

# Check the object looks right
ps_100_rarefied_medoid

# Save
saveRDS(ps_100_rarefied_medoid, "ps_100_rarefied_medoid_depth1000.rds")


# Build each component separately first
otu <- otu_table(medoid_otu_filtered, taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
cat("OTU table built:", dim(otu), "\n")

sdata <- sample_data_filtered
cat("Sample data built:", dim(sdata), "\n")

taxtab <- tax_table(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg)[surviving_asvs, ]
cat("Tax table built:", dim(taxtab), "\n")

tree <- prune_taxa(surviving_asvs, phy_tree(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg))
cat("Tree built, tips:", length(tree$tip.label), "\n")

# Now build phyloseq incrementally
ps_test1 <- phyloseq(otu, sdata)
cat("OTU + sample_data OK\n")

ps_test2 <- phyloseq(otu, sdata, taxtab)
cat("+ tax_table OK\n")

ps_test3 <- phyloseq(otu, sdata, taxtab, tree)
cat("+ tree OK\n")



#Prevalence filter single rarefied and 100 rarefied phyloseq using function below:
prevalence_filter <- function(phy, prev_frac = 0.1, min_reads = 10) {
  # Keep taxa that occur in > prev_frac fraction of samples AND have > min_reads counts
  phy_filt <- filter_taxa(
    phy,
    function(x) sum(x > min_reads) > (prev_frac * length(x)),
    prune = TRUE
  )
    
  # Identify and drop zero-count samples
  zero_samples <- sample_names(phy_filt)[
    rowSums(as(otu_table(phy_filt), "matrix")) == 0
  ]
  if (length(zero_samples) > 0) {
    message("Dropping ", length(zero_samples), " zero-count samples: ",
            paste(zero_samples, collapse = ", "))
    phy_filt <- prune_samples(!sample_names(phy_filt) %in% zero_samples, phy_filt)
  }
  
  return(phy_filt)
}


CANOE_prevfilt <- prevalence_filter(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg, 0.1, 1)

phy_rarefy100_prevfilt <- prevalence_filter(ps_test3, 0.1, 1)


#Determine how different ASV inclusion is, and show metric for variance in distribution between the three

```

### Prevalence Filter Results ###
```{r}
  # Apply 10% prevalence & >10 reads
phy_prev_10O_10R <- prevalence_filter(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg, prev_frac = 0.1, min_reads = 10)

  #pre: [ 14662 taxa and 891 samples ] 
  #post: (10% prevalence and >10 reads):  [  118 taxa and 883 samples ] *** Chosen moving forward ***
  #post: ( 5% prevalence and >10 reads):  [  237 taxa and 886 samples ] 
  #post: ( 5% prevalence and > 1 reads):  [  261 taxa and 886 samples ]
  #post: ( 1% prevalence and > 1 reads):  [ 1071 taxa and 890 samples ]
saveRDS(phy_prev_10O_10R, "~/Downloads/Prevalence_Phyloseq_10_10.rds")

# Now safely do TSS
phy_tss <- transform_sample_counts(phy_prev_10O_10R, function(x) x / sum(x))

### And log transform ONLY FOR RELATIVE ABUNDANCE TESTING!!! ###
otumat <- as(phyloseq::otu_table(phy_tss), "matrix")
pos_vals <- otumat[otumat > 0]
if (length(pos_vals) == 0) {
  stop("No positive values in OTU table; cannot compute pseudocount.")
}
pseudocount <- min(pos_vals, na.rm = TRUE) / 2
#pseudocount
phy_log_tss <- transform_sample_counts(phy_tss, function(x) log10(x + pseudocount))

```

```{r}
### Choosing best fit for data ###

# Step 1: Extract OTU table
otu_mat <- as(otu_table(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg_filter_10O_10R), "matrix")
if (taxa_are_rows(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg_filter_10O_10R)) {
  otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
}

# Step 2: Add pseudocount to avoid log(0)
otu_mat_pseudo <- otu_mat + 1e-6  # or use your previously calculated pseudocount

# Step 3: Apply CLR transformation
clr_mat <- compositions::clr(otu_mat_pseudo)  # Transforms each row

# Step 4 Reconstruct the phyloseq object with CLR-transformed data
otu_clr <- otu_table(clr_mat, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
phy_clr <- phyloseq(otu_clr, sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg_filter_10O_10R), tax_table(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg_filter_10O_10R))

otu_long_clr <- reshape2::melt(clr_mat)
colnames(otu_long_clr) <- c("Sample", "Taxon", "CLR_Abundance")

ggplot(otu_long_clr, aes(x = CLR_Abundance)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 100) +
  ggtitle("CLR-transformed abundance distribution") +
  xlab("CLR Abundance") +
  ylab("Frequency")


# Example for one taxon
taxon_name <- "ASV_1"  # replace with taxon of interest
df_lme_genus_test <- data.frame(
  Abundance = otu_mat[, taxon_name],
  LogAbundance = log10(otu_mat[, taxon_name] + pseudocount),
  #chronic_rhinitis12m = sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg_filter_10O_10R)$chronic_rhinitis12m,
  Age = sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg_filter_10O_10R)$Age,
  Subject_ID = sample_data(CANOE_phy_3Runs_noneg_filter_10O_10R)$Subject_ID
)
colnames(df_lme_genus_test)[1:2] <- c("Abundance", "LogAbundance")

# Model on raw TSS
model_raw_genus <- lmer(Abundance ~ Age + (1 | Subject_ID), data = df_lme_genus_test)

# Model on log-transformed TSS
model_log_genus <- lmer(LogAbundance ~ Age + (1 | Subject_ID), data = df_lme_genus_test)


# Create dataframe for CLR modeling
df_lme_clr <- data.frame(
  CLR_Abundance = otu_mat_clr[, taxon_name],
  #chronic_rhinitis12m = sample_data(phy_clr)$chronic_rhinitis12m,
  Age = sample_data(phy_clr)$Age,
  Subject_ID = sample_data(phy_clr)$Subject_ID
)

# Fit CLR model (no need for log-transform since CLR is already log-scale)
model_clr <- lmer(CLR_Abundance ~ Age + (1 | Subject_ID), data = df_lme_clr)

# Residual diagnostics for CLR model
par(mfrow=c(3,2))

# Residual plots for raw model
plot(resid(model_raw_genus), main="Residuals of raw TSS model")
qqnorm(resid(model_raw_genus), main="QQ plot raw TSS model")
qqline(resid(model_raw_genus))

# Residual plots for log model
plot(resid(model_log_genus), main="Residuals of log TSS model")
qqnorm(resid(model_log_genus), main="QQ plot log TSS model")
qqline(resid(model_log_genus))

#Residual plots for CLR model
plot(resid(model_clr), main="Residuals of CLR model")
qqnorm(resid(model_clr), main="QQ plot CLR model")
qqline(resid(model_clr))

# Model comparison including CLR
AIC(model_raw_genus, model_log_genus, model_clr)
BIC(model_raw_genus, model_log_genus, model_clr)

###Choosing 10% occurrance and > 10 reads because it includes the most abundant genus level ASVs while not inflating the total taxa present. Also choosing Log(TSS + pseudocount) as the phyloseq object to move forward with because of the lowest AIC, BIC values when plotting abundance lme. However, Beta Diversity will want only TSS, so will also make a cleanly named phyloseq object for that as well###

```

### Beta Diversity
```{r}

# 6 distances (Sørensen handled via presence/absence Bray)
dists <- c(
  "Weighted UniFrac"   = "wunifrac",
  "Unweighted UniFrac" = "unifrac",
  "Bray Curtis"        = "bray",
  "Sorensen"           = "sorensen",
  "Jaccard"            = "jaccard",
  "Canberra"           = "canberra"
)

set.seed(481516)
pnova_proj <- vector("list", length(dists))
proj_plt   <- vector("list", length(dists))
names(pnova_proj) <- names(dists)
names(proj_plt)   <- names(dists)

# Helper: get a distance object, returning a 'dist' for every method
get_dm <- function(phy, method_key) {
  if (method_key == "sorensen") {
    # Sørensen ≡ Bray on presence/absence
    vegan::vegdist(as(otu_table(phy), "matrix"), method = "bray", binary = TRUE)
  } else if (method_key == "jaccard") {
    # Jaccard on presence/absence
    vegan::vegdist(as(otu_table(phy), "matrix"), method = "jaccard", binary = TRUE)
  } else {
    # Methods supported directly by phyloseq::distance
    phyloseq::distance(phy, method = method_key)
  }
}

for (i in seq_along(dists)) {
  method_name <- names(dists)[i]   # pretty label
  method_key  <- dists[i]          # code string

  dm_group <- get_dm(phy_tss, method_key)

  # PCoA: pass the 'dist' object directly (works for all cases)
  pcoa <- ordinate(phy_tss, method = "PCoA", distance = dm_group)

  # PERMANOVA: use proper sample_data extraction
  md <- as(sample_data(phy_tss), "data.frame")
  pnova_proj[[i]] <- adonis2(dm_group ~ Age, data = md)

  plt <- plot_ordination(phy_tss, pcoa, type = "samples", color = "Group") +
    stat_ellipse(linetype = 2) +
    ggtitle(method_name) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    theme_bw() +
    annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf,
             label = paste('R^2==', round(pnova_proj[[i]]$R2[1], 3)),
             hjust = 1, vjust = 1, parse = TRUE, size = 3) +
    annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf,
             label = paste('P==', round(pnova_proj[[i]]$`Pr(>F)`[1], 3)),
             hjust = 1, vjust = 3.5, parse = TRUE, size = 3) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(color = "DarkBlue", size = 11, hjust = .5)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
      "child - newborn"  = "#E31A1C",
      "child - 4 months" = "#1F78B4",
      "child - 12 months"= "green"
    ))

  proj_plt[[i]] <- plt
}

# 2x3 grid with shared legend at bottom
BD_pcoa_plot <- ggarrange(
  plotlist = proj_plt,
  ncol = 3, nrow = 2,
  common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)
BD_pcoa_plot
```
